commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Human rebase and review are needed because the branch is dirty and must preserve current async message-cache persistence plus media/security dependency contracts.; Needs attention: The diff touches Telegram media download security and root dependency metadata, and the submitted branch is stale against current safer contracts.; Review finding: [P1] Preserve async message-cache persistence — extensions/telegram/src/me... (sha=b8ad83191132ea2cdb1c9a40312ded58f58444ba)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Telegram message-cache scoped indexes, Telegram media-resolution caching with stale-path eviction, focused Telegram tests, and a root `@openclaw/fs-safe` pin update.

PR surface: Source +258, Tests +77, Config 0, Other 0. Total +335 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction. The PR supplies after-fix live Telegram proof, while the current blockers come from source comparison against current main and the dirty merge state.
commands.review_followup: Human rebase and review are needed because the branch is dirty and must preserve current async message-cache persistence plus media/security dependency contracts.

target.title: [codex] Further optimize Telegram media and reply context flow
target.body:
## Summary

- add scoped indexes to the Telegram message cache so `recentBefore` and `around` avoid scanning and sorting the full cache
- cache successful Telegram media resolutions by file identity and relevant runtime options
- revalidate cached media paths before reuse so TTL cleanup cannot leave stale attachments behind
- add regression coverage for thread-scoped chat windows, media cache hits, and stale media cache eviction
- bump `@openclaw/fs-safe` to 0.2.1 so focused Telegram tests can install and resolve `@openclaw/fs-safe/config` cleanly

## Context

This builds on earlier Telegram-route performance work by `obviyus`, further optimizing the same flow by tightening reply-context lookups and avoiding repeated Telegram media hydration.

## Why

Telegram reply-context building can repeatedly query recent chat windows and hydrate the same reply media. This PR keeps those hot paths bounded while preserving the existing chat-wide and thread-specific behavior.

The media cache also checks the saved file still exists before returning a cached path, because long-running gateways can remove saved media through TTL cleanup.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Telegram reply-context lookups stay thread-scoped or chat-wide as requested, repeated Telegram media hydration reuses the cached saved path, and a deleted saved media file is evicted and re-downloaded instead of returning a stale path.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw gateway from this PR head (`b8ad83191132ea2cdb1c9a40312ded58f58444ba`) on macOS, running `OpenClaw 2026.5.10-beta.1` with the Telegram plugin loaded from the PR checkout. Telegram ingress used the real Bot API webhook for `@acecoderbot` through a temporary Cloudflare tunnel. The LLM provider was mocked only to make the response deterministic; Telegram update delivery, reply-context hydration, message-cache persistence, and reply dispatch all ran through the PR checkout.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran `pnpm openclaw --log-level debug gateway run --port 19043 --auth token --token [redacted] --bind loopback --verbose --ws-log compact` from the PR checkout with an isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, a real Telegram Bot API webhook, and a deterministic local OpenAI-compatible provider. Sent `PR80243 live seed OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_1` to `@acecoderbot`, then sent a real Telegram reply to that DM with `PR80243 live reply-context OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_2`. Also ran a local media/cache proof through `node --import tsx` using a real temporary state directory and local trusted Bot API file path.
- **Evidence after fix:** Redacted live runtime logs and persisted cache/model-input excerpts from the actual PR gateway run:

```text
2026-05-10T18:35:37.445+05:30 [telegram] [default] starting provider (@acecoderbot)
2026-05-10T18:35:38.067+05:30 [telegram] webhook local listener on http://127.0.0.1:8787/telegram-webhook
2026-05-10T18:35:38.562+05:30 [telegram] webhook advertised to telegram on [temporary-cloudflare-url]/telegram-webhook
2026-05-10T18:35:56.615+05:30 [telegram] update: {"message":{"message_id":10034,"from":{"id":"[sender-id]","is_bot":false},"chat":{"id":"[chat-id]","type":"private"},"text":"PR80243 live seed OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_1"}}
2026-05-10T18:35:56.649+05:30 telegram inbound: chatId=[chat-id] from=telegram:[sender-id] len=116 preview="[Telegram [sender] (@[username]) id:[sender-id] +1m Sun 2026-05-10 18:35 GMT+5:30] PR80243 live seed OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_1"
2026-05-10T18:35:58.620+05:30 [diagnostic] message processed: channel=telegram chatId=telegram:[sender-id] messageId=10034 sessionKey=agent:qa:main outcome=completed duration=1955ms
2026-05-10T18:36:07.756+05:30 [telegram] update: {"message":{"message_id":10036,"from":{"id":"[sender-id]","is_bot":false},"chat":{"id":"[chat-id]","type":"private"},"reply_to_message":{"message_id":10034,"text":"PR80243 live seed OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_1"},"text":"PR80243 live reply-context OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_2"}}
2026-05-10T18:36:07.768+05:30 telegram reply-context: replyToId=10034 replyToSender=[sender] replyToBody="PR80243 live seed OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_1"
2026-05-10T18:36:07.770+05:30 telegram inbound: chatId=[chat-id] from=telegram:[sender-id] len=253 preview="[Telegram [sender] (@[username]) id:[sender-id] +8s Sun 2026-05-10 18:36 GMT+5:30] PR80243 live reply-context OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_2\n\n[Reply chain - nearest first]\n[1. [sender] id:10034 ... PR80243 live seed OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_1"
2026-05-10T18:36:08.594+05:30 [diagnostic] message processed: channel=telegram chatId=telegram:[sender-id] messageId=10036 sessionKey=agent:qa:main outcome=completed duration=817ms

{"key":"default:[sender-id]:10034","node":{"sourceMessage":{"message_id":10034,"chat":{"id":"[chat-id]","type":"private"},"text":"PR80243 live seed OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_1"}}}
{"key":"default:[sender-id]:10036","node":{"sourceMessage":{"message_id":10036,"chat":{"id":"[chat-id]","type":"private"},"reply_to_message":{"message_id":10034,"text":"PR80243 live seed OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_1"},"text":"PR80243 live reply-context OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_2"}}}

{"model":"gpt-5.5","marker1":true,"marker2":true,"inputCount":7}
Conversation info: {"chat_id":"telegram:[redacted]","message_id":"10036","reply_to_id":"10034","has_reply_context":true}
Reply chain: [{"message_id":"10034","body":"PR80243 live seed OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_1"}]
Nearby reply target window:
#10034 [reply target] [sender]: PR80243 live seed OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_1
#10036 ->#10034 [sender]: PR80243 live reply-context OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_2
```

Supplemental local media/cache output:

```text
OpenClaw Telegram real behavior proof
thread_scoped_recent_before=42,43
chat_wide_recent_before=40,41,42,43
media_first_path_exists=true
media_second_reused_cached_path=true
getFile_calls_after_cache_hit=1
media_third_redownloaded_after_saved_path_deleted=true
getFile_calls_after_stale_path_eviction=2
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The live Telegram reply reached OpenClaw with `reply_to_message`, OpenClaw logged `telegram reply-context` for `replyToId=10034`, the inbound prompt included `has_reply_context: true`, the nearby reply target window included both the original DM and the reply, and both Telegram messages completed through the PR gateway. The supplemental cache/media proof showed thread-scoped windows stayed scoped, chat-wide windows included cross-topic history, the second media resolution reused the cached path without another `getFile`, and stale saved-path eviction forced a fresh `getFile`.
- **What was not tested:** No additional gaps for the live Telegram reply-context behavior. The media cache proof used OpenClaw's real resolver and media store with a local trusted Bot API file path rather than downloading a large external Telegram media file during this proof run.

## Validation

- `git diff --check`
- `CI=true pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --prefer-offline`
- `OPENCLAW_TEST_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_HELD=1 pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.resolve-media-retry.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`
target.author: safzanpirani

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
